mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead for agent-based applications. Ideal for organizations looking to streamline their AI agent deployments in production environments.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the deployment of Kagenti on your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> Install the CLI for Linux systems.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure gcloud CLI is installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig file to use the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to interact with your cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Kagenti application on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.4.0-alpha.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Deploy Kagenti in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes setting up monitoring and autoscaling for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Kagenti deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This removes the Kagenti release.
> This deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade your Kagenti installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.4.0-alpha.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti

```

> Drain all pods in the Kagenti namespace.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running and ready after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Helm chart version may not exist or the repository is not added.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Helm repo is added and the version is correct: 'helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may have configuration issues or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kagenti' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU/memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Kagenti deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Initial release with core functionalities for deploying AI agents, including lifecycle orchestration and security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti' with all pods in 'Running' status.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti
gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
